i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate بيئي agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — بيئي inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive المُبلّغ protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in التعدين communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and بيئي acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large اقتصاد ($450.1B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). High agricultural employment (11.2% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: إصلاح agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). عندما subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, المصلحة المتداخلة في monoculture between farmers and input suppliers يضعف.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce الاعتماد على النظام.

Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)

i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive المُبلّغ protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural تطويرment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water تطويرment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's بيئي regulatory agency, responsible for بيئي clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters اقتصاد ($671.4B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Services-dominant (71% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: تنفيذ front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information يضعف المصلحة المتداخلة في high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. إصلاحulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate الاعتماد على النظام.

i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian اقتصاد ($23.1B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with dominant gold التعدين sector (min=15.45% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold التعدين generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal التعدين), and community displacement. عندما بيئي and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: Mandate بيئي bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial التعدين licenses. عندما التعدين companies must pre-fund بيئي remediation and community compensation, المصلحة المتداخلة في extraction without accountability يضعف.

Publish لكل منجم بيئي impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to بيئي data makes W visible.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Open-pit gold التعدين inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce الاعتماد على النظام.

Document community health impacts near التعدين operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal التعدين activities and mercury use to provincial authorities

i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national بيئي assessment authority, responsible for بيئي impact assessments of التعدين projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees بيئي policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and المجتمع المدني space is restricted.

Major التعدين companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international التعدين standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for التعدين policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and التعدين technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on التعدين revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes بيئي obligations for التعدين companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable التعدين governance.

BG.s: عضو الاتحاد الأوروبي اقتصاد ($113.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the المصلحة المتداخلة between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. عندما Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The المصلحة المتداخلة breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory لكل محطة انبعاثات and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
غير قابل للكسر. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report انبعاثات violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)

اعتماد per-unit انبعاثات reporting aligned with EU taxonomy

i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional بيئي agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national بيئي monitoring agency collecting انبعاثات and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the مملوكة للدولة lignite التعدين company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent منظم الطاقة, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the المصلحة المتداخلة breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC اقتصاد. عندما financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, المصلحة المتداخلة في extraction breaks.
Publish لكل حقل بيئي and انبعاثات data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle انبعاثات visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report بيئي violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document انبعاثات or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include الطاقة المتجددة components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase الطاقة المتجددة allocation in investment portfolio
تنفيذ TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require انبعاثات disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: تعزيز بيئي standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation

Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
الضغط من أجل enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national انبعاثات reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's الهيئة البيئية, responsible for بيئي policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees بيئي impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the مملوكة للدولة oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited المُبلّغ protection; المجتمع المدني space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic تطويرment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a ملكية دستورية with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African اقتصاد ($21.5B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. عندما soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب from agricultural output data.
bp: إصلاح cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. عندما input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the المصلحة المتداخلة between cotton traders and input suppliers يضعف.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making بيئي and health costs visible makes W computable.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce الاعتماد على النظام — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)

Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's بيئي agency, responsible for بيئي impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of المجتمع المدني organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts بيئي monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the اقتصاد. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the المصلحة المتداخلة between BSP and the government in continued extraction يضعف — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. عندما non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, المصلحة المتداخلة في extraction breaks.
تنفيذ لكل حقل انبعاثات disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document انبعاثات data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation

i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of تطويرment, responsible for بيئي policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no المُبلّغ protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on تغير المناخ provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent اقتصاد ($54.9B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and التعدين (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). الزراعة عند 8.8% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي with soy expansion driving إزالة الغابات. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. عندما water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. عندما indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, المصلحة المتداخلة في extraction without consent يضعف.
Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making بيئي costs visible makes W computable from production data.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.

i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the التعدين administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing التعدين concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and بيئي rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state التعدين corporation overseeing Bolivia's التعدين sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's التعدين ministry, responsible for التعدين policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing بيئي policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian ديمقراطية. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island اقتصاد ($15.8B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. عندما beneficial ownership information and tax الشفافية data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: تنفيذ full beneficial ownership الشفافية in line with FATF recommendations. عندما the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through الشفافية, the المصلحة المتداخلة between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients يضعف.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.

i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas الخدمات المالية Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style برلماني ديمقراطية. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income اقتصاد ($19.4B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with diamond التعدين historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the المصلحة المتداخلة between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium يضعف — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. عندما non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, المصلحة المتداخلة في continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.
Publish full lifecycle بيئي data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all التعدين operations. Making بيئي costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Open-pit diamond التعدين inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term الاعتماد على النظام.

i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for بيئي policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for التعدين safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees التعدين policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the برلماني website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including التعدين.

BY.s: State-controlled اقتصاد ($76B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with significant manufacturing (20.3% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Potash التعدين (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash التعدين generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. عندما downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: International عقوبات (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the المصلحة المتداخلة by restricting Belaruskali's market access. عندما sanctioned export revenues fall below بيئي remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based بيئي monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making بيئي damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Potash التعدين inherently generates salt waste. However, solution التعدين and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document بيئي violations through secure channels to international بيئي organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand بيئي certification as condition of purchase (post-عقوبات)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian التعدين
Require independent بيئي audits as part of off-take agreements
International عقوبات bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export عقوبات until بيئي and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue بيئي performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international عقوبات on Belaruskali until بيئي and governance reforms are implemented
الدعوة من خلال UNECE for transboundary بيئي monitoring of potash التعدين impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for بيئي and governance reform
الضغط من أجل international بيئي damage assessment of Soligorsk التعدين district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, المجتمع المدني, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective المُبلّغ protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international بيئي organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's مملوكة للدولة potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK عقوبات since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under سلطوي governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU عقوبات framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including بيئي governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished اقتصاد ($71B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Mining dominates (28.81% of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. عندما supply chain traceability makes mine-level بيئي and labor conditions visible to end consumers, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. عندما artisanal miners must meet labor and بيئي standards to access global markets, المصلحة المتداخلة في unregulated extraction يضعف.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking لكل منجم بيئي and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal التعدين with proper safety and بيئي management can reduce الاعتماد على النظام compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report بيئي or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): تنفيذ full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
اعتماد IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
الاستثمار في formalization programs for artisanal التعدين communities
Ministry of Mines: إنفاذ 2018 Mining Code بيئي provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: تعزيز mineral certification to include بيئي and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal التعدين registration and oversight
تواصل مع Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement للمطالبة بـ enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code بيئي provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
الدعوة من خلال the African Union for continental responsible التعدين standards
الضغط من أجل OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale التعدين. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping التعدين sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees التعدين policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for التعدين sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent اقتصاد ($15.7B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the المصلحة المتداخلة between international operators and government revenue يضعف — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. عندما non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, المصلحة المتداخلة في continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
تنفيذ EITI-standard per-block revenue and انبعاثات disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to بيئي data makes W computable per production unit.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report بيئي violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: تنفيذ Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: تطوير economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
اعتماد per-block انبعاثات reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: تعزيز بيئي conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include بيئي impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
الدعوة من خلال CEMAC for regional بيئي standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with بيئي metrics
الضغط من أجل Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest اقتصاد in francophone West Africa ($87.1B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require إزالة الغابات-free supply chains — when per-farm إزالة الغابات data is linked to cocoa transactions, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. عندما cocoa buyers must verify zero-إزالة الغابات, the المصلحة المتداخلة between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm بيئي data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal إزالة الغابات for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): تنفيذ full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
الاستثمار في cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
اعتماد living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): إنفاذ traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to بيئي compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): تعزيز enforcement against إزالة الغابات in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
تواصل مع Député in the Assemblée nationale للمطالبة بـ enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
الدعوة من خلال the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding إزالة الغابات targets
الضغط من أجل living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end إزالة الغابات in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and بيئي regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to إزالة الغابات after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income اقتصاد ($330.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with massive التعدين sector (16.23% of الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium التعدين depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). عندما لكل منجم water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب from التعدين output data.
bp: تنفيذ water pricing that reflects true scarcity in التعدين regions. عندما miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, المصلحة المتداخلة في water-intensive extraction methods يضعف — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory لكل منجم water balance, tailings chemistry, and انبعاثات disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects بيئي data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report بيئي violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's المُبلّغ law (Ley 21.015) and بيئي complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
اعتماد Science Based Targets for انبعاثات reduction across التعدين operations
تنفيذ ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards

Sernageomin: تعزيز tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)

Support the Escazú Agreement on بيئي access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for التعدين supply chains
الضغط من أجل international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's بيئي enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with بيئي permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts بيئي impact assessments for التعدين projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's مملوكة للدولة copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper التعدين companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and التعدين service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees التعدين policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African اقتصاد ($53.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the المصلحة المتداخلة between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue يضعف — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. عندما REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, المصلحة المتداخلة في continued extraction breaks.
تنفيذ EITI-standard per-block بيئي disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to بيئي data makes W computable.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report بيئي violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable تطويرment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: تنفيذ بيئي monitoring and انبعاثات reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: تطوير post-oil diversification strategy
اعتماد TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): تعزيز بيئي conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: إنفاذ بيئي impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to بيئي data
تواصل مع Député in the Assemblée nationale للمطالبة بـ transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
الدعوة من خلال CEMAC for regional بيئي standards
الضغط من أجل EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees التعدين and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American اقتصاد ($418.8B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, المصلحة المتداخلة في fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in الطاقة المتجددة. عندما energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, المصلحة المتداخلة في continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory لكل حقل انبعاثات disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to انبعاثات makes W computable.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report بيئي violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
تنفيذ TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
الاستثمار في الطاقة المتجددة in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: تعزيز بيئي licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: إصلاح energy tariffs to accelerate الطاقة المتجددة deployment

Leverage OECD membership to advance بيئي governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's بيئي licensing authority, responsible for بيئي impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% مملوكة للدولة), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the منظم الطاقةy commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional بيئي authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income اقتصاد ($95.4B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي). Leader in ecotourism and بيئي policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. عندما per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: إصلاح agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. عندما export tax benefits require بيئي certification, the المصلحة المتداخلة between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production يضعف.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and بيئي complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): اعتماد pesticide reduction targets
الاستثمار في organic certification and agroforestry transitions
تنفيذ watershed-level بيئي monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): إصلاح export incentives to require بيئي certification
MINAE: تعزيز pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative بيئي impact assessments for monoculture expansion
تواصل مع Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa للمطالبة بـ pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's بيئي reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
الضغط من أجل green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts بيئي impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for بيئي services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global بيئي leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on بيئي access rights.

CU.s: Command اقتصاد ($107.4B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. عندما organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, المصلحة المتداخلة في conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that الاعتماد على النظام on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document بيئي degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: تعزيز بيئي monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
الدعوة من خلال CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
الضغط من أجل relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a دولة الحزب الواحد. Independent المجتمع المدني, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees بيئي policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island اقتصاد ($37.6B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with dominant services sector (76.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership الشفافية requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership الشفافية. عندما financial secrecy is eliminated, the المصلحة المتداخلة between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity يضعف.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's عضو الاتحاد الأوروبيship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.

i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an عضو الاتحاد الأوروبي with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial اقتصاد ($347B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with strong manufacturing (19.9% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). عضو الاتحاد الأوروبي. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). الخدمات عند 60.2% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite التعدين is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the المصلحة المتداخلة between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. عندما plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the المصلحة المتداخلة breaks.
Mandatory لكل محطة انبعاثات and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
غير قابل للكسر. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.

i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for بيئي law, conducting inspections and imposing عقوبات. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees بيئي policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% مملوكة للدولة), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite التعدين and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent منظم الطاقة. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic اقتصاد ($424.5B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. الخدمات عند 63.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: إصلاح agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. عندما the المصلحة المتداخلة between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

قابل للكسر جزئياً. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates الاعتماد على النظام can be reduced through circular nutrient management.

i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for بيئي regulation including agricultural انبعاثات and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — بيئي policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% انبعاثات reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest اقتصاد ($124.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and التعدين (2.04% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). الخدمات عند 59.8% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. عندما لكل منجم water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, يصبح W قابلاً للحساب.
bp: Mandate بيئي bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. عندما التعدين companies pre-fund cleanup, المصلحة المتداخلة في extraction without بيئي accountability يضعف.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from التعدين revenue.
قابل للكسر جزئياً. Open-pit التعدين disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce الاعتماد على النظام.

Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees التعدين policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs التعدين concessions and بيئي obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential ديمقراطية.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). الزراعة عند 14% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. عندما production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, المصلحة المتداخلة في continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). عندما solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, المصلحة المتداخلة في gas-fired power generation breaks.
تنفيذ لكل حقل انبعاثات disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes بيئي damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive المُبلّغ protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent اقتصاد ($124.7B الناتج المحلي الإجمالي) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). الزراعة عند 9.5% الناتج المحلي الإجمالي (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the المصلحة المتداخلة. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: تنفيذ the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. عندما democratic decisions override extraction interests, the المصلحة المتداخلة breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block بيئي impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's بيئي data (Amazon إزالة الغابات, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
غير قابل للكسر by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
